verdict: PASS — all six checks clear on second pass. Required sections 3, 4, 5, and 10 are present and non-trivial. Citation density across sections 5+6+7 is estimated at >85%, well above the 60% strict-mode threshold; the single primary resource [1] is cited throughout; no fake citations detected. No forward references: all technical terms are grounded in prior curriculum (weeks 11-12 Python, M1 probabilistic systems) or this topic's own introduced concepts, with back-references to 13.1/13.3/13.5 made explicit. Word count ~3370 falls within the standard-profile range of 3000–4400. No scope creep: corpus stays entirely within prompt-based format control; output validator pattern is an in-scope introduced concept; no API-level structured output features appear. Beginner readability passes: all introduced terms defined at first use, no jargon dumps, no hedging phrases, plain register throughout; one residual soft finding (KeyError in Introduction, prior Python curriculum) does not meet the 3-finding escalation threshold. All four issues flagged in pass 1 are resolved.
